 library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.2
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ purrr     1.1.0     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
Blood_count <- read_excel("smears_analysis_r.xlsx")
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `total_cell` = `[Cells] c/ml`
  )
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `LG_ratio_gt` = `LG_ratio-gt`
  )


``` r
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Shark_Id` = `Sharks Id`
  )
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `LG_ratio` = `L:G`
  )
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    total_count        = `total count`
  )
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure Antibody_titer is numeric
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(LG_ratio = as.numeric(LG_ratio))

# Check for NAs after conversion
sum(is.na(Blood_count$LG_ratio))
## [1] 8
# Calculate mean and SD per group per week
summary_data <- Blood_count %>%
  group_by(week, Groupe) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_LG_ratio = mean(LG_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_LG_ratio = sd(LG_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Create grouped barplot with error bars
grouped_barplot1 <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = factor(week), y = mean_LG_ratio, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_LG_ratio - sd_LG_ratio, ymax = mean_LG_ratio + sd_LG_ratio),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "L:G ± SD",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Mean L:G",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(grouped_barplot1)
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_bar()`).

Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  rename(RBC_WBC = `rbc:wbc`)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Check for NAs after conversion
sum(is.na(Blood_count$RBC_WBC))
## [1] 8
# Calculate mean and SD per group per week
summary_data_rbcwbc <- Blood_count %>%
  group_by(week, Groupe) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_RBC_WBC = mean(RBC_WBC, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_RBC_WBC = sd(RBC_WBC, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Create grouped barplot with error bars
grouped_barplot_rbcwbc <- ggplot(summary_data_rbcwbc, aes(x = factor(week), y = mean_RBC_WBC, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_RBC_WBC - sd_RBC_WBC,
                    ymax = mean_RBC_WBC + sd_RBC_WBC),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
                width = 0.2,
                color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "RBC:WBC ± SD",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Mean RBC:WBC",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(grouped_barplot_rbcwbc)
## Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_bar()`).

library(tidyverse)
library(car)        # For Levene's Test
## Loading required package: carData
## 
## Attaching package: 'car'
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     some
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     recode
library(lme4)
## Loading required package: Matrix
## 
## Attaching package: 'Matrix'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
library(lmerTest)   # For p-values in mixed models
## 
## Attaching package: 'lmerTest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:lme4':
## 
##     lmer
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     step
library(MuMIn)      # For R²
library(DHARMa)     # For model diagnostics
## This is DHARMa 0.4.7. For overview type '?DHARMa'. For recent changes, type news(package = 'DHARMa')
# Make sure your ratio column is numeric and factors are correctly set
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    RBC_WBC = as.numeric(RBC_WBC),     # rename if your column is `rbc:wbc`
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id) # replace with your shark ID column name
  )

# ----- 📊 1. Levene’s Test (homogeneity of variance) -----
leveneTest(RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor, data = Blood_count)
## Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (center = median)
##       Df F value Pr(>F)
## group  8  0.9903 0.4543
##       53
# ----- 🧪 2. Shapiro–Wilk Test (normality) -----
# Test normality of the raw RBC:WBC values
shapiro.test(Blood_count$RBC_WBC)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  Blood_count$RBC_WBC
## W = 0.97471, p-value = 0.2283
# (Optional visualization)
# ggplot(Blood_count, aes(sample = RBC_WBC)) + stat_qq() + stat_qq_line()

# ----- 📈 3. Fit GLMM -----
glmm_rbcwbc <- lmer(
  RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_count
)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
# ----- 📄 4. Model Summary + R² -----
summary(glmm_rbcwbc)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: 
## RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor)
##    Data: Blood_count
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 329.1
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.9365 -0.6611 -0.1223  0.6957  2.4601 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups          Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Shark_Id_factor (Intercept)  0.00    0.000   
##  Residual                    22.42    4.735   
## Number of obs: 62, groups:  Shark_Id_factor, 21
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                  Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)                       31.1668     1.7810  52.0000  17.499  < 2e-16
## Groupe_factorSummer               -1.3113     2.7019  52.0000  -0.485  0.62948
## Groupe_factorWinter              -11.4553     2.3673  52.0000  -4.839 1.21e-05
## week_factor6                      -2.2377     2.3673  52.0000  -0.945  0.34890
## week_factor8                      -7.0813     2.3673  52.0000  -2.991  0.00424
## Sex_factorMale                    -0.7734     1.2164  52.0000  -0.636  0.52770
## Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor6  -0.7840     3.8172  52.0000  -0.205  0.83807
## Groupe_factorWinter:week_factor6   3.0678     3.3479  52.0000   0.916  0.36372
## Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor8   4.8504     3.9655  52.0000   1.223  0.22679
## Groupe_factorWinter:week_factor8  10.9728     3.3479  52.0000   3.277  0.00187
##                                     
## (Intercept)                      ***
## Groupe_factorSummer                 
## Groupe_factorWinter              ***
## week_factor6                        
## week_factor8                     ** 
## Sex_factorMale                      
## Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor6    
## Groupe_factorWinter:week_factor6    
## Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor8    
## Groupe_factorWinter:week_factor8 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) Grp_fS Grp_fW wk_fc6 wk_fc8 Sx_fcM G_S:_6 G_W:_6 G_S:_8
## Grp_fctrSmm -0.567                                                        
## Grp_fctrWnt -0.665  0.438                                                 
## week_factr6 -0.665  0.438  0.500                                          
## week_factr8 -0.665  0.438  0.500  0.500                                   
## Sex_factrMl -0.341 -0.045  0.000  0.000  0.000                            
## Grp_fctS:_6  0.412 -0.706 -0.310 -0.620 -0.310  0.000                     
## Grp_fctW:_6  0.470 -0.310 -0.707 -0.707 -0.354  0.000  0.439              
## Grp_fctS:_8  0.412 -0.678 -0.298 -0.298 -0.597 -0.046  0.481  0.211       
## Grp_fctW:_8  0.470 -0.310 -0.707 -0.354 -0.707  0.000  0.219  0.500  0.422
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
r.squaredGLMM(glmm_rbcwbc)
##            R2m       R2c
## [1,] 0.4183612 0.4183612
# ----- 🧩 5. Residual diagnostics -----
simulation_output <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = glmm_rbcwbc, n = 250)
plot(simulation_output)

anova_rbcwbc <- aov(RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor, data = Blood_count)

# View the ANOVA table
summary(anova_rbcwbc)
##                           Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
## Groupe_factor              2  672.9   336.4  15.178 6.14e-06 ***
## week_factor                2   34.7    17.3   0.782    0.463    
## Groupe_factor:week_factor  4  267.0    66.8   3.012    0.026 *  
## Residuals                 53 1174.8    22.2                     
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 8 observations deleted due to missingness
# Optional: post-hoc pairwise comparisons if needed
TukeyHSD(anova_rbcwbc)
##   Tukey multiple comparisons of means
##     95% family-wise confidence level
## 
## Fit: aov(formula = RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor, data = Blood_count)
## 
## $Groupe_factor
##                        diff        lwr       upr     p adj
## Summer-Contrôle -0.03207382  -3.849795  3.785647 0.9997738
## Winter-Contrôle -6.77505614 -10.052169 -3.497943 0.0000207
## Winter-Summer   -6.74298231 -10.560703 -2.925261 0.0002458
## 
## $week_factor
##           diff       lwr      upr     p adj
## 6-4 -1.2557254 -4.759107 2.247656 0.6649708
## 8-4 -1.7827552 -5.329658 1.764148 0.4514560
## 8-6 -0.5270298 -4.073933 3.019873 0.9317790
## 
## $`Groupe_factor:week_factor`
##                              diff        lwr         upr     p adj
## Summer:4-Contrôle:4    -1.3886634 -10.066579  7.28925185 0.9998466
## Winter:4-Contrôle:4   -11.4552687 -19.066304 -3.84423357 0.0003421

## Winter:4-Summer:4     -10.0666053 -18.744521 -1.38869013 0.0120518
## Contrôle:6-Summer:4    -0.8490791  -9.526994  7.82883613 0.9999965

## Summer:8-Summer:6       0.6748310  -9.536444 10.88610609 0.9999998
## Winter:8-Summer:6      -3.1533017 -11.831217  5.52461347 0.9583621
## Contrôle:8-Winter:6     3.5439114  -4.067124 11.15494656 0.8479930
## Summer:8-Winter:6       6.8896039  -2.431972 16.21118012 0.3108094
## Winter:8-Winter:6       3.0614712  -4.549564 10.67250633 0.9267937
## Summer:8-Contrôle:8     3.3456925  -5.975884 12.66726868 0.9612263
## Winter:8-Contrôle:8    -0.4824402  -8.093475  7.12859489 0.9999999
## Winter:8-Summer:8      -3.8281327 -13.149709  5.49344354 0.9182996
library(tidyverse)

Blood_long <- Blood_count %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`[Rbc]`, `[Granulocyte]`, `[Thrombo]`, `[Lympho]`, `[GT]`, `[CE]`, `[FE]`),
    names_to = "Cell_type",
    values_to = "Count"
  )
ggplot(Blood_long, aes(x = week_factor, y = Count, fill = Groupe_factor)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Cell_type, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  labs(x = "Week", y = "Cell count", fill = "Group") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
## Warning: Removed 56 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).
## Warning: Removed 56 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

Blood_summary <- Blood_long %>%
  group_by(Groupe_factor, week_factor, Cell_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_value = mean(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(Blood_summary, aes(x = week_factor, y = mean_value, color = Groupe_factor, group = Groupe_factor)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_value - sd_value, ymax = mean_value + sd_value), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Cell_type, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  labs(x = "Week", y = "Mean cell count ± SD", color = "Group") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.

## Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).
